transcript.text: Hey, Will.
Hey, sorry I'm late.
No worries.
It's, you know, first Tuesday of the year.
I think there's a lot of us probably getting our heads back on from the holidays.
Yeah, yeah, absolutely.
Did you have a good break?
Did you take a break at all?
Yeah, it was great.
Definitely took a couple days off.
We just kind of hung out here in Chicago.
I've had a pretty busy year.
My wife has also been traveling a good amount recently.
So we just decided to kind of hang out, which was great.
Very relaxing.
Nice.
Yeah.
My husband and I had a blissfully boring holiday a couple of weeks too, which sometimes you just, you need downtime after, yeah, crazy life.
Totally.
Yeah, it was, yeah, it was nice.
Cool.
Good to hear.
I
Don't know if you had much chance to like go through all of the agenda.
There's a lot in here, but I think it's going to be pretty quick overall.
Just a few things that I wanted to flag.
Primarily looking ahead at my calendar and like aligning on our meeting schedule.
I wanted to meet this week since we had a couple of weeks post-holidays where we didn't chat.
And then starting next week, we'll meet and then move into that bi-weekly schedule, if that works for you guys.
Yeah, I think that's fine.
I think as long as we can, and you've been great and responsive via Slack.
I think that's totally fine.
Great.
Yeah.
And like, if anything comes up where we need to discuss, like I said, we can always hop back into a meeting one week.
Just freeze up a little bit of my time and yours and we can dig into smart level stuff each time.
No, I think we can probably do things async for a couple of weeks.
But yeah, if anything comes up, I'll just Slack you and we can hop on a call.
Perfect.
Slick.
So I had shared a lot of this.
Similar to the update I already shared.
So if you don't have any questions about kind of what's coming out of the pipe for you guys, I won't turn on about that too much.
One thing I did want to, a couple things.
So early in December, we had talked about Wednesdays giving you the JSON files and new docs.
I chatted with my content dev team, and it's going to be a little bit helpful if we can split that to Tuesday and Thursday.
So I wanted to check if it would be better for you guys to get the JSON files on Tuesday or the new Google Docs on Tuesday.
So you're saying the JSON files from the previous week?
Yeah.
And then new Google Docs for review on Thursday?
Correct.
So we can give either to you on Tuesday and then the other on Thursday.
I just didn't know if you wanted more time to publish the JSON files or more time to review the Google Docs.
on see
Um, so, okay, so that's a good question.
So I'll be kind of transparent here.
Like, the 10 or so that we had that I reviewed, like, it took a good amount of time to, like, review them because of, in some cases, I would say, like, inaccurate data.
There's only a couple of those.
But in other cases, just, like, not following the template that we had for the previous 10.
And so that admittedly, like, got me, like, a little bit concerned over the holidays when I'm like, okay, this is taking me a lot longer than I thought I would need to do to review.
Like, I had to review the full page instead of, like, reviewing the Metronome's take.
I hope that we will get better at that.
And if we can't get better at that, then I probably won't need as much time to review.
And maybe it will be only focused on, like, the Metronome's take part.
So that's kind of one more, like, broad statement.
Now,
I think given that, I probably would lean more towards having more time reviewing the Google Docs versus the JSONs.
And there will be hiccups.
It happens, so I'm not that worried about it.

But I would prefer to maybe have the Google Docs sooner for review, and I can try and prioritize that, getting those back to you quickly in case there's some back and forth, and then moving into the JSON file.
So I guess that would be, like, Google Docs Tuesday, JSON files Thursday, or something.
I don't know.
You tell me how that works.
Yeah, that was kind of how that split was working out in my brain, too.
I did escalate those issues that you had flagged with the template for my team, and we dug into that a little bit more today.
I think part of it was developing 10 different articles.
I know there's a few that slipped through that had the, like, common concerns that we had talked about removing and things.
So definitely escalated that high priority for us to fix that for you guys, because I don't want you to feel
Like you need to go line by line through, especially with SEO stuff.
Some of it's complex, right?
It's like, I'm understanding of that.
I think, again, like when I, my alarm bells start ringing when I'm like, okay, we'll discuss this and it's still kind of happening.
But it's all good.

And like even one thing that I've also been learning myself to be totally candid in the last three months is it's just like, if we are going to separate like an API versus like a consumer product page, like we need to have a level of consistency.
And like, and there's nuance.
So like 11 labs is an example is one that actually has a consumer developer and an API product.
However, like they actually have a really interesting model where they actually, they have the same price points for each one of their packages.
So it's like, does that make sense having two pages?
So like there, there is nuance there and I'm like, I definitely can recognize that.
But, I would maybe just ask you and the team to like, when we look at these.
let's let's say, say,
Just do that type of analysis and maybe just make sure that we have an understanding of why we're not promoting one page versus the other.
And again, I can help be helpful and like go through some of them, but it might be easier for you guys to try and catch those before we get them into Google Docs.
Totally.
And that was one thing that I had also escalated with my team.
And I noticed you had called out like XAI had an API page and things that slipped through.
And so in our content calendar that I put together, I'm having my team go through each of those and confirm that they are actually a singular article.
Or if they need a separate page to split that out so that that is accurately reflected before we even develop the content.
And yeah, if there are some where, you know, we think it makes sense to have two, but we want to check with you, I'll raise that for you and we can chat.
Yeah, just shoot me Slack and I'll like do a little bit of an investigation.
Again, think, yeah, so I think that's probably the big thing that I've noticed so far, but.
If there's other things that come up, we can kind of take them as is.
But it would be helpful, again, if you just do like another, you or the team just does like another quick spot check.
Okay, like this is the pricing model or like this is the pricing page and this is how they separate things out.
That's usually the best way to find out like consumer product versus like enterprise or like API type product.
Yeah, makes sense.

Related to that, kind of within like the JSON format and things that we've established, I just wanted to make sure that I and my team are really clear on the subheader copy that you had mentioned in your recent message.
So my understanding is it's this little blurb under the title that we would see in the tag and on the article.
I know in the JSON files that we had sent over, it's captured in that short description element.
Is there anything else?
And I know some of those didn't get into the Google Doc, but you mentioned you had been...
Adding them manually.
So I wanted to make sure that we're aligned there.
That's, yeah, that's all I was saying is that like, I think for this round, they are all on there.
So I think we're good.
I think it was like the first 10, they weren't on the Google Doc.
And so Chris and I had to kind of like manually add them after the fact, which is, again, it's fine.
It is what it is.
But for this round, yes, I think like they all should have subheaders.
And I think they were added and I just like edited them.
So it was more just like calling out, like, let's make sure that we have those in the Google Docs for everyone moving forward.
Totally.
Great.
I just want to make sure there wasn't a bigger piece of that picture that we were missing that you guys needed.
Well, no.
I mean, I think I noticed that this is like right after we launched them, that there, I think there was a, something happened like on our end where we didn't pull through the subheader.
We didn't pull the actual page, but that I think was more.

And it just, like, reminded me that, like, okay, we need to make sure that there's consistency within the copy on the Google Doc to the JSON, which, so yeah, it was more just a call based on something else.
Yeah, that makes sense.
And I think one of the glitches, I got some time to, like, play with the JSON files.
I'm not normally as hands-on, but sometimes I jump in.

And I was playing with some of the JSON conversions and noticing there's some pieces that we need to clean up that I've got my writing team and our eng team working on so that the Google Doc output matches what our JSON converter needs.
And, like, that, it can get a little bit messy because of how the JSON conversion works.
But I think we're getting really close to that.
So long story short, we should be needing less of your time there.

In terms of other revision patterns, also noticing there's some, like, some word choice things that are getting revised outside of metronome's take, we can definitely do a pass of the feedback that you've given.
On some of the recent batches to make sure that any like stylistic voice tone changes we're accounting for.
But if you've got any specific patterns in that realm that are top of mind, that are easy for you to call out, we can also take that and apply it moving forward too.
Yeah, I think it's fine.
think, again, like I think there's just some nuance like at the top of the pages where we actually talk about like the pricing model, the credit model, and the packaging model.
And there is like some blurredness of that, but I am capturing some of those things as well as the categorization in a separate spreadsheet.
So I'm working kind of on my end when I do the review to make sure that there's some consistency across like, yeah, just some consistency across those things.
And then as far as like the actual copy, like, I think it's like overall fine.
I think it's, yeah, I have.

I haven't noticed any like fundamental like style and tone things that I would change other than maybe I guess the one thing that I would say would probably be like in like pricing like the pricing analysis or like the product overview sections like maybe being just like a little bit more objective if we will and like kind of calling out like what it Versus like there's some where some wording where it was like a little bit like like there's like a revolutionary model or product.
It's like I think that's fine.
And maybe that's just like a personal taste.
But like I generally would anchor more on like this is what it is.
This is what it does.
And then like pricing analysis is again like what is it, what it is, what it does.
And then like the metronome's take is the opinion.
So like...
Based upon what this is and what it does, enables X, Y, Z, and that's the benefit to customers.
Again, that's how I think of it in my mind.
And so maybe that's, I didn't consciously make those edits to the tone, but maybe that's one area to think about.
But yeah, otherwise, nothing really comes to mind, I'd say.
Okay, cool.
That's helpful.
And yeah, we, our AI model, like AI models in general tend to be, yeah, overly complementary and promotional.
Yeah, it happens with LLMs, right?
It's like, so that's probably more of one thing just, yeah.
So maybe that's one thing to just keep in mind is like, yeah, is the model being a little bit too complementary or too effusive in some ways?
Yeah, that makes sense.
It's a good shout.
Any other pieces that are top of mind for you, like quality concerns or...
Things that you want to talk about before I jump into the last couple pieces?
No, I think we're good for now.
Cool.
I wanted to flag we are looking at scaling production because this is kind of a hybrid between PSEO and editorial with the Metronome's Take piece.
So this week we're looking at scaling to six.
I wanted to gauge your comfort level one because I know you're still reviewing a lot of these as we finalize that alignment.
Long-term scaling, I'm sort of envisioning hitting somewhere between like six to eight pieces a week.
So I wanted to gauge your comfort with getting six moving forward for the next couple of weeks and then we can continue to scale from there.
Yeah.
Yeah, I mean, that's totally fine by me.
Again, I think our – I'm all for scale.
I think I just want to make sure like the – I'm like the quality control piece of the operation.
So I want to make sure that there's a high level of quality.
And if there is, then we can go to six.
It's amazing.
Like I should have
We again, feasibly to review more as we get better and there's a higher quality on the first draft, less edits and revisions.
So I'm fine with that.
But I think if we have this conversation in two weeks and it's like I'm still spending the same amount of time reviewing, I'm probably not going to want to go to more than Fathom.
Totally.
Yeah, that makes sense.
So I've got six in the queue for this week.
If for whatever reason you can only review five, like totally fine.
We can just like pass a piece on to the next week.
And then, yeah, we'll sort of we'll hang at six for now and see and then we can reevaluate like early next week or in a couple weeks.
Okay.
And what is the ideal timing for you to get those back?
If we get those back on like Friday, end of day or Monday, so we've got them first thing Monday, it's pretty easy for us to turn around those days.
Based on files, and have them to you by that Thursday.
Okay.
And do you have a, where are you going to be submitting the Google Docs?
Is it in, we have like a folder in Drive, right?
Yeah, so there's a folder in Drive, and I will send a message in Slack when like the batch is ready.
Okay.
you can also see them, there's a couple links here in this Airtables View section.
Okay.
So in this by status view here, yeah, it's got all of those lined up.
And it will have the Google Doc.
So like when these are in production, when they're ready for you, your Google Doc will be here in this column.
Okay.
We try to make it as easy as possible if don't have to fish through a bajillion Google folders.
Yeah, no problem.
Oh, okay.
Oh,
I'm just, like, making a reminder here.
Yeah, no worries.
Okay.
Okay, yeah, so I've blocked some time on Thursday, but I might not get to all of them.
So, yeah, I'll say, like, hopefully, like, Monday or Friday morning.
Well, on Thursday, I think our goal when we set those dates, we usually like to have those over to you by, end of day.
And so this week, because we've got the 10 JSON files we're converting, this week, we're looking at sending over those 10 JSON by Wednesday, end of day.
And then by Thursday, end of day, we'll have the new Google Docs, which I realize is a slight delay.
Okay.
But it's just because we want to prioritize those 10 pieces to get them published.
Okay, no problem.
Yeah, just, yeah, if you can just make sure that you Slack me or just, like, Slack the channel.
Yeah.
That'd be helpful.
Yeah, totally.

And similar, I think you guys, you let me know when you would run through all the other JSON files, but just as Slack on your end, once you publish the pieces, because we then go through and grab those URLs and we have a Looker dashboard set up for us to start doing some initial reporting, looking at like traffic and general impressions and things and going through like indexing to make sure that all of that's looking kosher.
Okay.
Yeah, that works for me.
Awesome.
Okay.
Yeah.
All right.
Yeah, that should work.
And then otherwise, I know you said that you have a spreadsheet in the works for some of the, like that top level pricing and categorization.
At any point when you're ready to share that, we're happy to take it and we can start to figure out the best way for us to implement.
But it's like putting the category at the top of the Google doc.
And if that needs to be in the JSON file somewhere, I'm not sure how you guys are handling categorization.
At this point.
Sorry, can you say that again?
Yeah, no worries.
We had talked a few weeks ago that you guys are still deciding on how you want to add tags or categories to each piece of content.
We have not been tagging them yet because we didn't have that breakdown from you all.
I know with the 10 pieces that were published, I saw some tags on them.
Yeah, so let's do this another, let's do this like this week's round and see how it breaks down because it's kind of like what I was saying before where there's nuance.
It's not been like the easiest way to like bucket everything.
So let me take another look at this week and then maybe I can share this spreadsheet with you and we can go a little bit deeper.
Sure.
Okay.

Because, again, it's like, you know, we have, we could have multiple pages for multiple companies, and then on top of that, like, they maybe have some nuance in how they price one product versus the next.
So, but I haven't looked at it in a couple weeks, but I sent it to Chris to, like, kind of, like, test this out and see how it fits.
I think it looks okay for right now, but for this round, I'll take another look and see where these fit, and then, you we can go from there.
Perfect.
Yeah, makes sense.
Like you said, I know there's, there's a lot of nuance between all of this, and much as it's nice to break things down into clean buckets, it doesn't always happen that way.
Yeah, yeah, and there's, like, yeah, so I'll leave it at that, yeah.
Cool.

And we already talked about starting with ByLadyChats, I just put basically what that schedule would look like here, and I'll update our content, our calendar invite as well, so that that's clear for everyone.
So, that's all I had for this week.
Anything else on top?
I think that's good.
mean, obviously like want to continue to push these out now that we've got the first 10 or so up.
So, you know, just keep cracking away.
Great.
Yeah.
It was excited to see them go live.
Cool.
Awesome.
Awesome.
We'll appreciate it.
And we'll talk to you in a couple of weeks.
Sounds good.
Have a good one.
You too.
See ya.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Align on content workflow, quality, and production scale.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**New Workflow:** Google Docs will be delivered Tuesdays for review, with JSON files following on Thursdays. This gives Metronome more time for the critical review phase.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=252.0)
  - [**Quality Control:** Metronome's review time is high due to inconsistent templates and inaccurate data. GrowthX is implementing new checks to fix these issues and reduce the review burden.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=304.0)
  - [**Production Scale:** Production will increase to 6 articles/week, contingent on quality improving. If review time doesn't decrease, the rate will be capped at 5.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=908.0)
  - [**Tone & Categorization:** Content analysis sections must be objective ("what it is"), reserving opinion for the "Metronome's Take." A new categorization system is in development to provide consistency.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=769.0)

## Topics

### Content Quality & Review Process

  - [**Problem:** Metronome's review time is excessive because articles often deviate from the agreed-upon template and contain inaccurate data.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=304.0)
  - [**Example:** The 11 Labs page incorrectly split a single product into two pages (consumer/API) despite its unified pricing model.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=453.0)
  - [**Solution:** GrowthX will implement new checks to ensure:](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=498.0)
      - [Singular articles are not split into multiple pages.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=498.0)
      - [Subheaders are included in Google Docs to eliminate manual additions.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=564.0)
      - [JSON conversion is accurate to prevent data mismatches.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=673.0)

### New Workflow & Schedule

  - [**Rationale:** To give Metronome more time for the critical review phase, the delivery schedule is being split.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=252.0)
  - [**New Cadence:**](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=252.0)
      - [**Tuesdays:** New Google Docs for review.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=252.0)
      - [**Thursdays:** JSON files for publication.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=252.0)
  - [**This Week's Exception:** The 10 articles from the previous batch will be prioritized for publication.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=1104.0)
      - [**Wed, EOD:** 10 JSON files delivered.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=1104.0)
      - [**Thurs, EOD:** New batch of Google Docs delivered.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=1104.0)

### Production Scaling

  - [**Plan:** Increase production from 5 to 6 articles/week.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=908.0)
  - [**Contingency:** The 6-article rate is dependent on quality improvements. If review time remains high, the rate will be capped at 5.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=908.0)
  - [**Review Cadence:** Reviews are due Friday EOD or Monday AM.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=1007.0)

### Tone & Categorization

  - [**Tone:** Analysis sections must be objective ("what it is"), reserving opinion for the "Metronome's Take." This corrects the AI's tendency for overly promotional language.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=769.0)
  - [**Categorization:** A new system is needed to provide consistency across articles.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=1175.0)
      - [**Challenge:** The system must account for product nuance (e.g., API vs. consumer) and varied pricing models.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=1227.0)
      - [**Status:** Will is developing a spreadsheet to define categories and will share it with GrowthX for implementation.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=1175.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=1104.0)
      - [Deliver 10 JSON files by Wed, EOD.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=1104.0)
      - [Deliver new Google Docs by Thurs, EOD.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=1104.0)
      - [Notify Metronome via Slack when docs are ready.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=1026.0)
      - [Update calendar invites for the bi-weekly meeting cadence.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=1304.0)
  - [**Metronome:**](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=1007.0)
      - [Review new Google Docs by Fri, EOD or Mon, AM.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=1007.0)
      - [Notify GrowthX via Slack after publishing JSON files.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=1141.0)
      - [Share the categorization spreadsheet with GrowthX for implementation.](https://fathom.video/share/_T2SKdVKb_7PNaZNkkgj1Y4HL49Hisd1?tab=summary&timestamp=1175.0)

action_items.description: Update recurring invite to bi-weekly starting next week
Deliver 6 new Google Docs to Will by Jan 8 EOD; Slack channel on delivery
Deliver 10 JSONs to Will by Jan 7 EOD; Slack channel on delivery
Review this week's batch; share categorization spreadsheet w/ Katya
